title: Git symlinks (mode 120000) required for cross-platform support
description: Windows, Linux, git-bash, and GitHub all handle symlinks differently. Git's native symlink mode (120000) ensures compatibility across all platforms without special handling.
root_cause: Initial symlink implementation used OS-level calls (mklink, ln -s) which don't work consistently across platforms and don't show as symlinks on GitHub.
solution: Use git commands to create symlinks instead of OS-level tools. Set `core.symlinks = true` in git config to enable proper symlink creation.
lesson_summary: Always use git-native symlinks (mode 120000) for cross-platform projects. This works seamlessly in Windows (git-bash), Linux, WSL, macOS, and displays correctly on GitHub.

code_pattern.create: ln -s source target  # Works uniformly across all platforms when core.symlinks=true
code_pattern.verify: git ls-files --stage | grep '^120000'  # Shows symlinks in git index

implementation.file1: ~/.bashrc - Added global git config for all shells
implementation.file2: bin/.gitbashrc - Added git config for git-bash-specific initialization
implementation.file3: bin/agence - Updated ^init command to document symlink strategy

references: nexus/logs/LOGS.json - Log entries for symlink creation
